# tests/fixtures/forbidden-literals.txt
#
# Forbidden literals in skills/**/*.md — single source of truth shared
# by tests/test-skill-conformance.sh (CI deny-list) AND
# hooks/warn-config-drift.sh (real-time WARN). See plan
# plans/SKILL_FILE_DRIFT_FIX.md Phase 4 and Phase 1's
# references/canonical-config-prelude.md for the format spec.
#
# Format:
#   - One entry per line. Comments (lines starting with `#`) and blank
#     lines are skipped.
#   - Default: literal substring match (grep -F). The substring's
#     presence anywhere on a line counts as a hit.
#   - Prefix `re:` flips the entry to extended-regex (grep -E). The
#     pattern is unanchored unless it contains its own anchors. Authors
#     of `re:` entries are responsible for adding `^`/`$`/word-boundary
#     anchors as needed.
#
# When adding a new config field whose value could appear hardcoded in a
# skill .md, append the literal here. Both the deny-list test and the
# drift-warn hook pick it up at runtime — no code change needed.
#
# Note on re: prefix collision (refine-2 R2.5/DA2.22): forbidden literals
# starting with the substring `re:` cannot be deny-listed in literal
# mode (the prefix would be stripped). Currently no entry triggers this.
# Future-proof workaround: express as a regex with literal `re:` in the
# pattern, e.g. `re:re:remote` denies the literal substring `re:remote`.

TZ=America/New_York
npm run test:all
npm start
re:\$TEST_OUT/\.test-results\.txt
